The '''TIE Fighter Construction Facility''' was a massive [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]. Dedicated to the overwhelming mass production of [[TIE series/Legends|TIE craft]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]], the facility was considered a prime target for those seeking to strike a blow against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]

==Characteristics==
[[File:Control_Room.jpg|thumb|left|The facility's control room.]]
These factories were constructed throughout the galaxy to maintain and build up the Empire's military–industrial complex and typically hung in low orbit above a planet. They were owned and operated by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]], though ultimate ownership laid with the Empire. It was protected by several squadrons of well trained [[Imperial Navy commando]]s. This facility was one of the first of its type.<ref name="TFUgdb">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' {{C|Databank}}</ref>

Inside the facility, the Empire began perfecting its mass production techniques which churned out thousands of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN fighters]]. Rows upon rows of TIE components parts moved along a magnetic conveyor where automated laser arms welded the vehicles together before they were transported to loading bays.<ref name="TFUg" />

==History==
[[File:TIEfacility_tfu.jpg|thumb|right|250px|TIE facility concept art]]
In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], the facility was taken over by former [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Rahm Kota/Legends|Rahm Kota]] and his [[Kota's Militia|comrades]]. During the [[Battle of the TIE Fighter Construction Facility|ensuing battle]], Kota's men planted explosives throughout the factory. This was, however, not their main objective, as Kota sought to draw out [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], but instead Vader's [[Galen Marek|secret apprentice]] was drawn to the conflict. Kota detached the base's command center by detonating some of those explosives in a bid to kill the apprentice, but failed in his efforts to achieve the latter.<ref name="TFUg" />

==Behind the scenes==
The TIE Fighter Construction Facility appears in the [[2008]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'', in which it is the first story-based level. In-game, the base is referred to as the "TIE Fighter Factory", and much of its information is provided by the game's Databank. The facility also appears in the [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|novel]] and [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (graphic novel)|comic]] of the same name.

The interior of the Facility was designed having "Imperial" visuals, derived from and based on the sceneries of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] and the rest of the [[original trilogy]]. Story-wise its layout shows the construction of TIE fighters, with wing racks and cockpit assembly lines. Gameplay-wise it was furnished with objects that could be hurled against enemies, or destroyed (in some platforms exemplifying the Digital Molecular Matter technology). The command center went under several revisions, and the final color palette was chosen to complement the green-emerald effects around Rahm Kota.<ref name="Art of TFU">''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

The first appearance of the Facility was in a preliminary demo delivered in February [[2007]] where a player could explore its first areas. Thanks to its complex layout and the usage of Force Powers, the game's high interactivity could be demonstrated. In the later demo, the Facility's infuser room was used an example of the lighting engine.<ref name="Art of TFU" />

The TIE Fighter Construction Facility has differing appearances between versions of ''The Force Unleashed'' video games. In the Wii/PS2 versions, the facility's external appearance has an array of pods adjoined together on a spine-like structure, with the top having a long bridge area. In the PS3 and XBOX360 versions of the game, the structure is largely more sleeker and also curved, almost bell-shaped.
